description_long: This is a small, wall-mounted holy water font, typically found at the entrance of churches or religious buildings. The backplate is made of a reddish-brown stone or plaster, with a white marble slab attached to it, featuring a simple engraved cross at the top center. The basin is semi-circular and scalloped, crafted from white marble, and is designed to hold a small amount of water. The marble shows some discoloration and wear, indicating frequent use. The overall design is minimalistic, with the cross being the only decorative element. The object is compact and intended for individual use rather than as a large public fountain.
description: A small, wall-mounted marble holy water font with a scalloped basin and an engraved cross on a white marble backplate, set against a reddish-brown wall.
description_view_0: Front view showing the white marble backplate with an engraved cross and the scalloped marble basin attached to a reddish-brown wall.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the shallow depth of the basin and the thickness of the backplate against the wall.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain reddish-brown backplate with no visible decorative features or openings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the profile of the marble basin and the layered construction of the backplate.
attribute_ornamentation_details: The only decorative element is a simple engraved cross at the top center of the white marble backplate. The basin itself is scalloped, adding a subtle ornamental touch. There are no additional motifs, sculptures, or engravings.
attribute_basin_structure: The basin is semi-circular with a scalloped rim, made of white marble, and is shallow, designed to hold a small amount of water. There are no visible tiers or compartments.
attribute_jet_or_spray_pattern: No visible jets or spray patterns; the basin is intended for still water only.
